params.text:
The values behind good care: the 6 Cs

The 6 Cs are a widely used set of values that describe what good care looks like in practice. They remind everyone working in health and social care of the behaviours that people who draw on care have a right to expect. Tap each card below to explore what each of the 6 Cs means for your day-to-day work.

params.alt: A care worker with a person they support

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: The 6 Cs of care — the values expected of everyone who works in health and social care.

dialogs.answer: Caring consistently about the people you support and making a positive difference to their lives, delivered in a way that is right for each individual.
Delivering care with kindness, empathy, dignity and respect — seeing the person, not just the task.
Having the knowledge and skills to meet a person’s needs, and keeping that competence up to date through training and reflection.
Listening as well as speaking. Good communication with people, families and colleagues keeps care safe and builds trust.
Doing the right thing and speaking up when something is wrong or when someone may be at risk, even when it feels difficult.
Dedication to the people you support and to improving the quality of their care and your own practice over time.
